metadata.title: Installation Guide
metadata.description: How to install the CrossPoint Reader Korean firmware on Xteink X3/X4. Multiple methods are supported, including the Web Flasher, SD card, and esptool.
metadata.ogDescription: How to install the CrossPoint Reader Korean firmware on Xteink X3/X4.

hero.title: Installation Guide
hero.description: Learn how to install the CrossPoint Reader Korean firmware on Xteink X3/X4. A single firmware supports both models, and you can choose from multiple installation methods including the Web Flasher, SD card, and OTA.

method1.title: Method 1: Use Web Flasher (Recommended)
method1.description: This is the easiest and fastest method. You can flash the firmware directly from the Web Flasher on this site.

step1.title: Connect via USB
step1.x4: X4: Connect to your computer with a USB-C cable.
step1.x3: X3: Connect to your computer using the included magnetic charging/data cable.
step1.note: Note: X3 packages with a serial number sticker may not be detected over USB. In that case, use Method 2: SD Card Update below.

step2.title: Open the Web Flasher
step2.description: Navigate to the Web Flasher page. Please use Chrome or Edge.

step3.title: Flash the Firmware
step3.bullet1: Select the USB serial device in the device picker

step4.title: Apply the Firmware
step4.description: The device reboots automatically when flashing completes. If it appears stuck:
step4.item1: Press the Reset button on the device once
step4.item2: Hold the Power button for at least 1 second to boot

method2.title: Method 2: SD Card Firmware Update
method2.description: Use this method when USB connection is unreliable or wireless updates are not feasible. Supported from v1.2.0-ko.15, this method can also be used to roll back to the X3 store firmware.
method2.newUserTitle: A. First-time users (X3 OEM firmware where USB is not working)
method2.newUserStep2: Copy it to the root folder of your SD card and rename it to update.bin.
method2.newUserStep3: Insert the SD card into the device.
method2.newUserStep4: While holding down the left side button, hold the power button to boot.
method2.newUserStep5: The OEM bootloader will automatically detect and flash the firmware.
method2.existingUserTitle: B. Existing users (already running CrossPoint Reader-KO)
method2.existingUserStep1: Download {filename} from the GitHub releases and copy it to your SD card.
method2.existingUserStep2: Insert the SD card into the device.
method2.existingUserStep3: Go to Settings → System → SD Card Firmware Update.
method2.existingUserStep4: Select the firmware file on the SD card and flashing will start automatically.
method2.existingUserStep5: The device will reboot automatically when flashing completes.
method2.existingUserTip: Tip: From the same menu you can also select a downloaded store firmware (X3/X4 OEM) to roll back.
method2.otaTitle: Note: OTA (WiFi) updates are available from the same menu
method2.otaDescription: If WiFi is connected, go to Settings → System → Update to perform an OTA update without any extra files. Both SD and OTA paths use raw partition writes plus direct otadata updates so they work on the X4 silicon.

method3.title: Method 3: Download Firmware and Use a Flasher
method3.description: Download the firmware file directly and flash it using a Web Flasher.
method3.step1Description: Download the {filename} file from the GitHub releases page.
method3.step3Description: In the OTA fast flash controls section:
method3.step3Item1: Click Select File and choose the downloaded .bin file
method3.step3Item2: Click Flash firmware from file to start flashing

method4.title: Method 4: Use esptool (Advanced)
method4.description: Flash the firmware directly from the command line. This method uses the merged binary file.
method4.step1Description: Download the {filename} file from the GitHub releases page. (Merged binary specifically for esptool.)
method4.cautionText: Do not unplug the USB cable while flashing. Doing so may damage the device.

revert.title: Restore the Original Firmware (Store Firmware)
revert.description: To return to the original Xteink official firmware, use one of the methods below. You can download the latest store firmware for X3/X4 directly here.
revert.settingsNote: When switching between CrossPoint firmware and store firmware, all settings (WiFi, reading preferences, etc.) are preserved unchanged.
revert.filenameNote: Filenames retain the original OEM names. After downloading, flash using one of the methods below.
revert.option1Suffix: lets you flash the X3 or X4 store firmware directly.
revert.option2Description: Copy the downloaded store firmware file onto your SD card, then go to Settings → System → SD Card Firmware Update and select the file to flash automatically.
